mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns during stabilization periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 245 indicate entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary policy expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 245 consistently coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in real GDP combined with falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices exceeding 105 indicate capacity utilization approaching peak efficiency, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to margin expansion expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 105 consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns.
